name: Oxytocin improved aged muscle stem-cell activation in mice
summary: Systemic oxytocin improved aged mouse skeletal-muscle regeneration and enhanced muscle stem-cell activation/proliferation.
statement: In aged mice, systemic oxytocin rapidly improved skeletal-muscle regeneration after injury while enhancing aged muscle stem-cell activation and proliferation through MAPK/ERK signaling.
population_or_model: Aged mice and skeletal-muscle injury/regeneration assays
time_horizon: Short-term systemic hormone treatment around muscle injury and repair assessment
quantitative_note: The staged baseline treats this as animal functional support for a circulating niche-support factor, not as human efficacy.
caveats: The efficacy evidence is in mice rather than older humans.
Oxytocin has broad endocrine and behavioral biology that may complicate chronic preventive use.
Muscle injury repair does not establish durable whole-body healthy-aging benefit.
